cards.prompt: The policy of extending a nation's power through territorial acquisition, economic domination, or political control over other regions

cards.prompt: The ideology that justified European imperialism as a moral duty to 'civilize' non-European peoples

cards.prompt: The economic system in which industrial nations extracted raw materials from colonies and sold manufactured goods back to them, keeping colonies dependent

cards.prompt: Battle of Adwa — Ethiopia defeats Italy

cards.prompt: The 1899–1902 war in South Africa between British forces and Boer settlers, notable for British concentration camps for Afrikaner civilians

cards.prompt: The British colonial government that ruled India from 1858 to 1947 following the dissolution of the East India Company

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The economic policy that forced Indian farmers to grow raw materials for British industry rather than food crops, contributing to famines

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Migrations of millions of workers from India, China, and other colonial regions to work on plantations and in mines after the abolition of slavery

cards.prompt: The migration of millions of workers from southern and eastern Europe to the Americas between 1880 and 1920

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The working class in industrial capitalist societies who sell their labor for wages, as described by Karl Marx

cards.prompt: Organizations of workers that bargained collectively with employers for better wages, hours, and working conditions

cards.prompt: The ideology calling for government control of major industries to reduce inequality, distinct from Marxist revolution

cards.prompt: The rapid growth of cities driven by industrialization, as people moved from rural areas to factory towns

cards.prompt: The 1884–1885 conference at which European powers divided Africa among themselves without African input

cards.prompt: The social reform movement in Britain that organized working-class political representation and sought universal male suffrage

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Cecil Rhodes
cards.answer: British imperialist who colonized southern Africa (Rhodesia), advocated British expansion, and dreamed of a Cape-to-Cairo railway across Africa

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The international trade network that relied on steam-powered ships and telegraph communications in the 19th century, accelerating global interdependence

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1850–1864 Chinese uprising against the Qing Dynasty led by Hong Xiuquan, who claimed to be the brother of Jesus, killing 20 million people

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Qing Dynasty's humiliating treaties with Western powers after military defeats that forced China to open ports and grant extraterritoriality to foreigners

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1839–1842 war between Britain and China fought over Britain's right to sell opium in China, which Britain won, forcing China to open ports

cards.prompt: First Opium War between Britain and China

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1898–1901 Chinese nationalist uprising against foreign influence and Christian missionaries, crushed by an eight-nation military force

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Monroe Doctrine
cards.answer: 1823 US policy declaring that European powers should not intervene in the Western Hemisphere, asserting US dominance in the Americas

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Japan's victory over Russia in 1905 that shocked the world by demonstrating that an Asian nation could defeat a European power

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The movement for women's right to vote that gained momentum in the 19th century in Britain, the United States, and other industrialized nations

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The global ecological impact of industrialization including deforestation, soil depletion, and air and water pollution from factories

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Spanish-American War, which gave the US control of Cuba, Puerto Rico, and the Philippines

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The late 19th-century competition among European powers to colonize and divide Africa, resulting in 90% of Africa under European control by 1914

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Berlin Conference that divided Africa among European powers

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The economic relationship in which colonies provided raw materials and markets for the manufactured goods of industrialized colonizers

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The justification for European imperialism claiming that it was Europeans' moral duty to spread civilization, Christianity, and progress to non-European peoples

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The agricultural system in which farmers grow crops for export rather than local consumption, tied to global markets and colonial economic needs

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Indentured labor
cards.answer: A system in which workers agreed to work for a set period (typically 5 years) in exchange for transportation; used after slavery's abolition to provide cheap plantation labor in the Caribbean and elsewhere

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1860s-1890s movement of Chinese reformers who wanted to modernize China militarily while preserving Confucian values

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The rapid industrialization, urbanization, and political consolidation of Germany following unification in 1871

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The improved railroad, steamship, and telegraph infrastructure of the 19th century that transformed global trade and communication

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Opium War between Britain and China

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The legally enforced areas of economic privilege granted to foreign powers in China, exempting them from Chinese law

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: An unequal trade arrangement imposed on China after the Opium War that required China to open ports, fix tariffs, and grant extraterritorial rights to foreigners

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 19th-century social movement in Europe demanding workers' rights, factory reforms, and improved living conditions resulting from industrialization

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The British abolition of the slave trade and then slavery throughout the British Empire, motivated by both humanitarian and economic arguments

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Britain abolishes slavery throughout the British Empire

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The system of forced cultivation in the Dutch East Indies (Indonesia) requiring farmers to grow export crops on 20% of their land for the colonial government

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Social Darwinism
cards.answer: The misapplication of Darwin's evolutionary theory to human society, claiming that European domination proved the natural superiority of European peoples; used to justify imperialism

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 19th-century movement advocating for voting rights for women, particularly prominent in Britain and the United States

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Cecil Rhodes
cards.answer: British imperialist who dominated the diamond and gold industries in South Africa, colonized Rhodesia (Zimbabwe/Zambia), and advocated for British imperial expansion throughout Africa

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The political ideology claiming that each ethnic or cultural group deserves its own independent state, driving the breakup of multiethnic empires in the 19th–20th centuries
